name: Paul Williams
description: US blues and R&B saxophone player and composer (1915-2002)
abstract: Paul "Hucklebuck" Williams (July 13, 1915 – September 14, 2002) was an American jazz and blues saxophonist, bandleader, and songwriter. His record "The Huckle-Buck", recorded in December 1948, was one of the most successful R&B records of the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Paul 'Hucklebuck' Williams came through there, needed a bass player... I got on the bus and left with Paul 'Hucklebuck' Williams. OK. Now everybody in Paul Williams band is--is--is--raw men.

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Brown
about_quotes.evidence: I had an experience and when my book comes out it will speak of an occasion in which we were going to Gulfport, Mississippi — Charles Brown, and Paul “Hucklebuck” Williams and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Brown interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995
